title: O Willie Brew'd.
content:
Tune - "Willie brew'd a peck o' maut."


I.

        O, Willie brew'd a peck o' maut,
            And Rob and Allan came to see:
        Three blither hearts, that lee-lang night
            Ye wad na find in Christendie.
                    We are na fou, we're no that fou,
                        But just a drappie in our e'e;
                    The cock may craw, the day may daw,
                        And aye we'll taste the barley bree.

II.

        Here are we met, three merry boys,
            Three merry boys, I trow, are we;
        And mony a night we've merry been,
            And mony mae we hope to be!

III.

        It is the moon--I ken her horn,
            That's blinkin in the lift sae hie;
        She shines sae bright to wyle us hame,
            But, by my sooth, she'll wait a wee!

IV.

        Wha first shall rise to gang awa',
            A cuckold, coward loon is he!
        Wha last beside his chair shall fa',
            He is the king amang us three!
                    We are na fou, we're no that fou,
                        But just a drappie in our e'e;
                    The cock may craw, the day may daw,
                        And aye we'll taste the barley bree.
meaning:
Robert Burns's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "O Willie Brew'd."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
